coder_1.comments: The parents were trying to model to the child how to say "no," so there is the possibility that this is mimickry and nothing else. However, based on the parents' reactions, it seems that the child is upset about what the parents said, prohibiting the state of affairs that they describe.

The child seems to be referring to the lexical item "no" as a possible name for an object, corresponding to exclusion. Another possibility is that the child is referring to a lack of the object (nonexistence) or denying a proposition involving the object

The child seems to be mimicking the mother. Another possibility is that the child is affirming the mother's proposition that they are not afraid, though this is made less likely by the repetition being only the middle portion of the relevant phrase

Child seems to be prohibiting the parent from cooking the given food (since child doesn't like it); could also be rejecting eating the food, but at the time of the utterance the cooking is occuring so prohibition seems more likely
